questions.question: What is a Constitution?
questions.correct_answer: A set of fundamental rules and principles that guide how a country is governed.
questions.explanation: It defines the structure of government and the rights of citizens.
questions.source_section: Why Does a Country Need a Constitution?

questions.question: Why does a country need a Constitution?
questions.correct_answer: To provide rules for governance and protect citizens’ rights.
questions.explanation: It limits the power of government and ensures fairness and equality.
questions.source_section: Why Does a Country Need a Constitution?

questions.question: When did the Constitution of India come into effect?
questions.explanation: This day is celebrated as Republic Day in India.

questions.question: What is federalism?
questions.correct_answer: A system of government where power is divided between the central and state governments.
questions.explanation: Each level of government has its own responsibilities and authority.

questions.question: What is a parliamentary form of government?
questions.correct_answer: A system where the executive is responsible to the legislature.
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister and Council of Ministers are accountable to Parliament.

questions.question: What does separation of powers mean?
questions.correct_answer: Division of government powers among legislature, executive and judiciary.
questions.explanation: This prevents concentration of power and ensures balance in governance.

questions.question: What are Fundamental Rights?
questions.correct_answer: Basic rights guaranteed to all citizens by the Constitution.
questions.explanation: They ensure freedom, equality and justice in society.

questions.question: What does secularism mean in the Indian Constitution?
questions.correct_answer: The state treats all religions equally and does not promote any religion.
questions.explanation: Citizens are free to practice and follow any religion.

questions.question: Who was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: He played a major role in drafting the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the role of the judiciary in the Constitution?
questions.correct_answer: To interpret laws and protect the rights of citizens.
questions.explanation: Courts ensure that laws follow the Constitution.

notes.title: Constitution
notes.content: A constitution provides the basic rules for governing a country and protects the rights of its citizens.

notes.title: Federalism
notes.content: In India, power is divided between the central government and state governments to manage the country effectively.

notes.title: Parliamentary System
notes.content: The government is responsible to the Parliament and must maintain the confidence of the majority of members.

notes.title: Fundamental Rights
notes.content: These rights protect citizens’ freedom and equality and allow them to live with dignity.

notes.title: Secularism
notes.content: India has no official religion and all religions are treated equally by the state.

facts.fact: The Constitution of India is the longest written constitution in the world.
The Constitution came into effect on 26 January 1950.
Dr. B.R. Ambedkar is known as the chief architect of the Indian Constitution.
The Constitution guarantees Fundamental Rights to all citizens.
